root_text.sn47.7:1.1: “Atthi, bhikkhave, himavato pabbatarājassa duggā visamā desā, yattha neva makkaṭānaṁ cārī na manussānaṁ.
root_text.sn47.7:1.2: Atthi, bhikkhave, himavato pabbatarājassa duggā visamā desā, yattha makkaṭānañhi kho cārī, na manussānaṁ.
root_text.sn47.7:1.3: Atthi, bhikkhave, himavato pabbatarājassa samā bhūmibhāgā ramaṇīyā, yattha makkaṭānañceva cārī manussānañca.
root_text.sn47.7:1.4: Tatra, bhikkhave, luddā makkaṭavīthīsu lepaṁ oḍḍenti makkaṭānaṁ bādhanāya.
root_text.sn47.7:2.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, ye te makkaṭā abālajātikā alolajātikā, te taṁ lepaṁ disvā ārakā parivajjanti.
root_text.sn47.7:2.2: Yo pana so hoti makkaṭo bālajātiko lolajātiko, so taṁ lepaṁ upasaṅkamitvā hatthena gaṇhāti.
root_text.sn47.7:2.8: ‘Ubho hatthe mocessāmi pādañcā’ti dutiyena pādena gaṇhāti.
root_text.sn47.7:2.10: ‘Ubho hatthe mocessāmi pāde cā’ti tuṇḍena gaṇhāti.
root_text.sn47.7:2.12: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, makkaṭo pañcoḍḍito thunaṁ seti anayaṁ āpanno byasanaṁ āpanno yathākāmakaraṇīyo luddassa.
root_text.sn47.7:2.13: Tamenaṁ, bhikkhave, luddo vijjhitvā tasmiṁyeva kaṭṭhakataṅgāre avassajjetvā yena kāmaṁ pakkamati.
root_text.sn47.7:2.14: Evaṁ so taṁ, bhikkhave, hoti yo agocare carati paravisaye.
root_text.sn47.7:3.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, mā agocare carittha paravisaye.
root_text.sn47.7:3.2: Agocare, bhikkhave, carataṁ paravisaye lacchati māro otāraṁ, lacchati māro ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn47.7:3.3: Ko ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno agocaro paravisayo?
root_text.sn47.7:3.7: Cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā,
root_text.sn47.7:3.11: kāyaviññeyyā phoṭṭhabbā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn47.7:4.1: Gocare, bhikkhave, caratha sake pettike visaye.
root_text.sn47.7:4.2: Gocare, bhikkhave, carataṁ sake pettike visaye na lacchati māro otāraṁ, na lacchati māro ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn47.7:4.3: Ko ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno gocaro sako pettiko visayo?
root_text.sn47.7:4.7: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.7:4.10: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.7:4.11: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno gocaro sako pettiko visayo”ti.

translation_text.sn47.7:1.1: “Mendicants, in the Himalayas there are regions that are rugged and impassable. In some such regions, neither monkeys nor humans can go,
translation_text.sn47.7:1.2: while in others, monkeys can go but not humans.
translation_text.sn47.7:1.3: There are also level, pleasant places where both monkeys and humans can go.
translation_text.sn47.7:1.4: There hunters lay snares of tar on the monkey trails to catch the monkeys.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.1: The monkeys who are not foolhardy and reckless see the tar and avoid it from afar.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.2: But a foolish and reckless monkey goes up to the tar and grabs it with a hand.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.4: Thinking to free his hand, he grabs it with his other hand.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.6: Thinking to free both hands, he grabs it with a foot.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.8: Thinking to free both hands and foot, he grabs it with his other foot.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.10: Thinking to free both hands and feet, he grabs it with his snout.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.12: And so the monkey, trapped at five points, just lies there screeching. He’d meet with tragedy and disaster, and the hunter can do what he wants with him.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.13: The hunter spears him, pries him off that tarred block of wood, and goes wherever he wants.
translation_text.sn47.7:2.14: That’s what happens when you roam out of your territory into the domain of others.
translation_text.sn47.7:3.1: So, mendicants, don’t roam out of your own territory into the domain of others.
translation_text.sn47.7:3.2: If you roam out of your own territory into the domain of others, Māra will find a vulnerability and get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn47.7:3.3: And what is not a mendicant’s own territory but the domain of others?
translation_text.sn47.7:3.5: It’s the five kinds of sensual stimulation.
translation_text.sn47.7:3.7: Sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn47.7:3.11: Touches known by the body, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn47.7:3.12: This is not a mendicant’s own territory but the domain of others.
translation_text.sn47.7:4.1: You should roam inside your own territory, the domain of your fathers.
translation_text.sn47.7:4.2: If you roam inside your own territory, the domain of your fathers, Māra won’t find a vulnerability and get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn47.7:4.3: And what is a mendicant’s own territory, the domain of the fathers?
translation_text.sn47.7:4.5: It’s the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.7:4.7: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.7:4.8: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.7:4.10: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.7:4.11: This is a mendicant’s own territory, the domain of the fathers.”

variant_text.sn47.7:2.13: tasmiṁyeva kaṭṭhakataṅgāre → tasmiṁyeva makkaṭaṁ uddharitvā (bj, sya-all)
variant_text.sn47.7:2.14: Evaṁ so taṁ → evaṁ hi taṁ (bj, pts1ed); evaṁ hetaṁ (sya-all)
